steps.title: Use the angle relationships
steps.content: From the parallel-line diagram, angles $a$ and $c$ are equal, and angles $b$ and $c$ form a linear pair. Therefore, $a+b=180^\circ$.

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
Substitute the expressions for $a$ and $b$. This uses the fact that the two angle measures together make a straight angle.
$$(4k+57)+(\frac{k}{4}+55)=180$$
$$4k+\frac{k}{4}+112=180$$
$$\frac{17k}{4}=68$$
$$k=16$$
